summary: In a graduation keynote address, Whitney Wolfe Herd advises students to cultivate three essential forms of connection: to themselves, to a higher power, and to others. She emphasizes the importance of trusting one's intuition over societal expectations and provides a practical exercise for setting long-term intentions by writing a letter to one's future self.

principles.statement: Cultivate a deep connection with yourself.
principles.rationale: It is the longest relationship you will ever have. Befriending yourself ensures you will never feel alone when life gets difficult or scary.
principles.source_quote: have a deep connection with yourself. Because that is really the longest relationship you will have.

principles.statement: Listen to and trust your intuition over societal expectations.
principles.rationale: Intuition is a secret language that helps you discern what is right for you, even when society or well-meaning family members try to point you in different directions.
principles.source_quote: I am here because I listened to my intuition. I trusted myself and I knew the difference between what was right for me and what was right for somebody else.

principles.statement: Lean on a connection to a higher power.
principles.rationale: Life is confusing and hard. Believing in something bigger than yourself is the only thing that can truly carry you through unimaginable events.
principles.source_quote: the only thing that really can carry you through is a belief in something higher and bigger than ourselves.

frameworks.name: Future-Self Intention Setting
frameworks.when_to_apply: When starting a new chapter in life and needing to clarify long-term dreams and goals.
steps: Get an envelope, a piece of paper, and a pen.
Write your name and a date far in the future (e.g., 18 years from now).
Put yourself in that future present moment and write down what you have achieved, where you have gone, and how you have connected with yourself, others, and a higher power.
Seal the envelope and open it when you reach that target age.
frameworks.source_quote: I want you to write a letter to your future selves... put yourself in that present moment and tell yourself where you are. What have you achieved?

mental_models.name: The Three Pillars of Connection
mental_models.description: Viewing a fulfilling and resilient life as being supported by three essential connections: connection to self (intuition), connection to a higher power (resilience), and connection to others (community).
mental_models.example: She structures her core life advice to graduates around these three specific pillars, explaining how each serves a distinct purpose in navigating life's challenges.

heuristics: If you can dream it, you can do it.
Set the intention to make it happen.

signature_quotes.text: Real connection is not on the phone. It's in your hearts. It's in your spirit. It's with each other.
signature_quotes.context: Concluding her speech on the true meaning of connection.

signature_quotes.text: I am here because I listened to my intuition. I trusted myself and I knew the difference between what was right for me and what was right for somebody else.
signature_quotes.context: Explaining how she achieved success despite not being the most technically experienced person.

signature_quotes.text: If you can learn how to befriend yourself right now, right here... I promise you, you will never feel alone.

anti_patterns.description: Seeking connection primarily through phones and devices.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It distracts from real connection, which happens in the heart, spirit, and through genuine human interaction.

anti_patterns.description: Blindly following the directions of society or family.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It ignores your own intuition and leads you away from what is uniquely right for you.
